title: Automating Enterprise Data Matching Workflows @ Yext

blocks.text: Built and scaled a data matching automation workflow at Yext to reduce manual enterprise onboarding work, improve data accuracy, and accelerate client activation across publisher networks. Saved 20,000+ work hours annually, eliminated ~$1M in operating cost, and maintained 96% CSAT.
Problem
Enterprise onboarding required manual matching across Google, Yelp, Tripadvisor, and Yahoo.
Teams reviewed thousands of rows by hand to identify accurate matches.
Data discrepancies delayed client activation and platform syncing.
No scalable workflow existed for repeatable data validation.
My Role
Identified the operational bottleneck and learned Python to prototype the MVP.
Partnered with Engineering and Data teams to scale matching logic and accuracy.
Worked with Sales and CS to align the workflow with client onboarding timelines.
Product Approach
Targeted a high-volume, rules-based workflow with clear automation upside.
Started in healthcare provider data where accuracy and compliance mattered most.
Generalized the matching pattern to additional industries after proof of value.
Preserved manual review paths to keep team trust in automated output.
Solution

items.title: Automated matching logic
items.description: High-confidence matching across large publisher datasets.

items.title: Validation workflows
items.description: Onboarding-ready data validation tuned per enterprise client.

items.title: Exception handling
items.description: Ambiguous records routed to operators with clear context.

items.title: Repeatable patterns
items.description: Reusable workflow templates across industries and publishers.

items.title: Match quality reporting
items.description: Visibility into accuracy, throughput, and manual review load.

blocks.text: Key Product Decisions

blocks.tradeoff: Automated only high-confidence matches; preserved manual review for ambiguous cases.
blocks.outcome: Accuracy stayed high while manual load dropped sharply.

blocks.tradeoff: Invested in exception handling before pushing for full adoption.
blocks.outcome: Teams trusted output enough to use it on production onboarding.

blocks.tradeoff: Shipped a Python MVP first, then partnered with Engineering to harden it.
blocks.outcome: Earned investment from technical teams with proof of value.

blocks.tradeoff: Started narrow in healthcare, then generalized the workflow.
blocks.outcome: Same pattern scaled to multiple industries and clients.

blocks.text: Results & Impact
Saved 20,000+ work hours annually through automated matching.
Eliminated approximately $1M in annual operating cost.
Maintained 96% CSAT while accelerating onboarding.
Faster enterprise client activation across publisher networks.
Improved data accuracy and platform reliability.
Reduced manual intervention via standardized confidence-based routing.
Metrics Tracked
Work hours saved
Operating cost reduction
Match accuracy
Manual review rate
Enterprise onboarding cycle time
CSAT
Key Learnings
Best automation opportunities are repetitive, rules-based, and high-volume.
Lightweight prototypes earn engineering investment when they prove real value.
Automation only scales when teams trust the output and the exception path.
